raw_extraction.innovation: Gato is a single, large-scale transformer sequence model trained to handle diverse modalities (text, images, proprioception, actions) and tasks (dialogue, image captioning, Atari gaming, robot manipulation) using a unified autoregressive framework with prompt conditioning
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Simulated control tasks (e.g., Meta-World, BabyAI, DM Control Suite, ALE Atari), vision-language datasets (e.g., MassiveText, ALIGN, Conceptual Captions, M3W), robotics datasets (RGB Stacking simulator and real robot)
raw_extraction.application: Multi-modal task execution, dialogue, image captioning, Atari gameplay, robot manipulation (e.g., block stacking), instruction following, real-world control
raw_extraction.license: Not specified in paper (likely open-source or proprietary)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Building a single generalist agent capable of handling multiple tasks across text, vision, and physical interaction without task-specific models or hand-crafted policies
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with multimodal tokenization
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning on specific tasks using demonstrations (e.g., robotics stacking, image captioning)
raw_extraction.extension: Data is serialized into a flat sequence of tokens across modalities, enabling a single model to process text, images, and actions as a unified sequence with task-inferred context via prompt conditioning
